nodes.name: HestonPathSampler
nodes.description: Simulates correlated Monte Carlo paths for both the stock price (S) and instantaneous variance (v) under the Heston stochastic-volatility model. Discretises the coupled SDEs dS = sqrt(v)·S·dW_S and dv = kappa·(theta - v)·dt + sigma_v·sqrt(v)·dW_v with correlation rho between the two Brownian increments, producing num_sims full trajectories over [0, T] with step size dt.

inputs.name: rho
inputs.constraints: -1 <= rho <= 1; Brownian correlation between price and variance processes

inputs.name: T
inputs.constraints: T > 0; total simulation horizon in years

inputs.name: num_sims
inputs.constraints: num_sims >= 1; number of independent Monte Carlo paths

outputs.name: S_paths
outputs.constraints: all entries > 0; stock price trajectories from S0

outputs.name: v_paths
outputs.constraints: all entries >= 0; variance trajectories from v0

nodes.type_signature: (S0: float, v0: float, rho: float, kappa: float, theta: float, sigma_v: float, T: float, dt: float, num_sims: int) -> tuple[float array [num_sims, n_steps+1], float array [num_sims, n_steps+1]]
nodes.conceptual_summary: CorrelatedBivariateMeanRevertingDiffusionEnsembleSampler. Jointly discretises two coupled Itô stochastic differential equations over a uniform time grid using an Euler–Maruyama scheme, producing an ensemble of independent sample paths for two co-evolving scalar state variables: one following a scale-multiplicative geometric diffusion (log-normally distributed increments, strictly positive) and one following a square-root mean-reverting diffusion (CIR/Feller process, non-negative with an absorbing or reflecting boundary at zero). The two Wiener increment streams are linearly correlated via a scalar correlation coefficient, implemented through a 2×2 Cholesky decomposition of the instantaneous covariance matrix applied at each time step. The result is a batch of [num_sims × (n_steps+1)] trajectory matrices for each state variable, capturing the joint distributional evolution of the coupled system.. Applications: Neuroscience – joint simulation of membrane potential (geometric-like excursions) and synaptic conductance (mean-reverting toward a resting level) in a single-compartment neuron model, where the two processes are negatively correlated (increased conductance dampens voltage excursions), generating ensembles of correlated spike-drive trajectories for population coding analysis, Climate & Earth Systems Modelling – coupled simulation of a positive-definite atmospheric tracer concentration (multiplicative dispersion) and a mean-reverting turbulent diffusivity field (square-root CIR dynamics), with positive correlation encoding the feedback between concentration gradients and eddy mixing rates, producing trajectory ensembles for uncertainty quantification in climate projections, Epidemiology – simultaneous path sampling of a susceptible-population scalar (geometric depletion) and a mean-reverting time-varying transmission rate (CIR dynamics representing seasonal forcing with noise), with correlation encoding behavioural feedback loops between disease prevalence and contact-rate adaptation, enabling stochastic R₀ trajectory ensembles for outbreak forecasting, Materials Science / Rheology – correlated simulation of a polymer chain end-to-end distance scalar (multiplicative geometric diffusion under applied strain) and a mean-reverting internal relaxation modulus (square-root Feller process), with negative correlation capturing the Weissenberg effect, producing ensembles of viscoelastic response trajectories for Monte Carlo stress-relaxation analysis
